changes: Modified write_meta() to compute blake3 checksum before serialization
Added checksum computation logic: serialize with checksum=None, hash, then include in final output
Changed serialization from pretty-printed JSON to compact form for canonical representation
Added comprehensive write_meta_checksum() test with 50+ lines validating checksum presence, format, and correctness

patterns_discovered.description: Serialize twice: first with checksum=None for hashing, then serialize again with computed checksum for storage. Avoids circular dependency.
Use serde_json::to_vec() (compact) not to_vec_pretty() to eliminate whitespace ambiguity in canonical form for deterministic hashing
Clear checksum field, recompute hash, compare to stored value to detect corruption. Test validates this pattern.

gotchas_discovered.impact: Using pretty-printed JSON would cause checksum mismatches on re-verification. Must use compact form.

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: blake3::hash() returns Hash object, not string
gotchas_discovered.impact: Direct storage would fail type checking. Must convert via .to_hex().to_string()

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Checksum field must be None during hashing
gotchas_discovered.impact: Including the checksum in its own hash creates circular dependency. Must serialize without it first.
gotchas_discovered.resolution: Clone meta, set checksum=None, hash that, then set computed value

approach_details.compliance_notes: Implements the meta.json corruption detection pattern specified in storage-decision.md: checksum field detects corruption, fallback to JSONL replay on mismatch

recommendations.suggestion: Verify that read_meta() implements checksum validation and JSONL replay fallback as mentioned in context. This write_meta() is the write-half; read half needs matching validation.
Add inline comments documenting blake3 hash format (64 hex characters) and canonical JSON requirement for maintainability
Consider edge case test: corrupted checksum field in JSON should be detected on read_meta() - validates round-trip integrity
